# Cache Driver Interface

Any custom cache driver must implement this interface. All built-in drivers follow this contract.

## Required Methods

| Method                       | Description                            |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `options`                    | Driver options object                  |
| `name`                       | Driver name (string)                   |
| `removeNamespace(namespace)` | Remove all cached items by namespace   |
| `setOptions(options)`        | Set the driver options                 |
| `parseKey(key)`              | Parse the cache key (string or object) |
| `set(key, value, ttl?)`      | Set a value in the cache               |
| `get(key)`                   | Get a value from the cache             |
| `remove(key)`                | Remove a value from the cache          |
| `flush()`                    | Flush the entire cache                 |
| `connect()`                  | Connect to the cache backend           |
| `client`                     | (optional) Underlying client instance  |
| `disconnect()`               | (optional) Disconnect from backend     |

## Example

```ts
export interface CacheDriver<ClientType, Options> {
  options: Options;
  name: string;
  removeNamespace(namespace: string): Promise<any>;
  setOptions(options: Options): any;
  parseKey(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<string>;
  set(key: string | GenericObject, value: any, ttl?: number): Promise<any>;
  get(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<any | null>;
  remove(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<void>;
  flush(): Promise<void>;
  connect(): Promise<any>;
  client?: ClientType;
  disconnect?(): Promise<void>;
}
```

## Extension Points

- Extend [BaseCacheDriver](./base-cache-driver) for common logic
- Use [parseCacheKey](./utils#parse-cache-key) for key normalization

See [Make Your Own Cache Driver](./make-your-own-cache-driver) for a full example.
